rulings.Case Title: FERGUSON ENTERPRISES, INC. VS. AMERICAN HOME MAKEOVERS, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 20, 2011, line 6, PLAINTIFF FERGUSON ENTERPRISES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO MAINTAIN ITS SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION DOES NOT PRESENT EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATING HOW FERGUSON HAS STANDING TO SUE ON THE CREDIT APPLICATION. THAT AGREEMENT IS EXPRESSLY BETWEEN DEFENDANT AND WOLSELEY AND THERE IS NO EVIDENCE REGARDING THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN FERGUSON AND WOLSELEY. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 20, 2011, line 2, PLAINTIFF M SAMUELS Motion For Order To Compel Answers And Responses By Defendant Ralph Tashjian To 1) Plaintiffs First Form Interrogatories - General; 2) Plaintiff's First Reqeust For Production Of Documents; To Deem Admitted By Defendant The Matters Specified In Plaintiff's First Requests For Admissions To Defendant To Compel Responses To Plaintiff's First Requests For Admissions To Defendant; Awarding Plaintiff Monetary Sanctions In The Sum Of $1,540 As Reasonable Attorney Fees And Costs For Having To Bring These Motions. rulings.Case Title: SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION, LOCAL 1021 VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Service Employees International Union, Local 1021 Notice And Motion For Petition To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 20, 2011, line 12, Petitioner Service Employees International Union, Local 1021 Motion For Petition To Compel Arbitration. PETITIONER'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS DENIED. PETITIONER CANNOT MEET ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT THE PRESENT DISPUTE (PETITIONER'S TERMINATION) IS SUBJECT TO ARBITRATION BECAUSE PETITIONER CANNOT CITE TO ANY SPECIFIC PROVISION ON THE CBA THAT REQUIRES ARBITRATION OF THIS ISSUE. MOREOVER, THE EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT PETITIONER WAS HIRED INTO A TEMPORARY POSITION FROM A NEAR LIST AND THAT SHE WAS NOT BEING RETURNED AS PERMANENT EMPLOYEE. THEREFORE, PETITIONER CANNOT MEET ITS BURDEN AS TO THE ARBITRABILITY OF THIS DISPUTE. =(302/LMG)
